title: 11 Herbal Creams For Menopause
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great help during menopause. They often contain herbs like valerian root, black cohosh, and oat straw that can ease hot flashes and night sweats. Valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) is known for its calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that come with menopause. Black cohosh (Cimicifuga racemosa) is often used to balance hormone levels and alleviate symptoms like mood swings and vaginal dryness. Oat straw (Avena sativa) is rich in antioxidants and can help soothe and calm the nervous system, leading to a better sleep and reduced stress levels. These herbal creams can be applied to the skin, allowing the active ingredients to be absorbed and work from the inside out. By using herbal creams, women can find natural relief from menopause symptoms and improve their overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone as its active constituents. These compounds have a sedative and calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause. The valerenic acid in Valeriana officinalis creams has been shown to bind to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. By reducing stress and promoting a sense of calm, Valeriana officinalis creams can help regulate hormonal imbalances and improve sleep quality. This, in turn, can lead to a reduction in hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), 2 tablespoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root powder, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler until warm. Add the dried Valeriana officinalis root powder and mix until well combined.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the warm oil mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5-10 minutes. The mixture will start to thicken.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, the Valeriana officinalis cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams contains a combination of active constituents, including sesquiterpenes, lignans, and phenylpropanoids. These compounds work together to help reduce symptoms associated with menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats. The sesquiterpenes, specifically, have been shown to have a positive effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which helps regulate hormone balance. The lignans and phenylpropanoids in Cimicifuga racemosa creams also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate menopausal symptoms like mood swings and joint pain. By addressing these underlying issues, Cimicifuga racemosa creams can provide relief for women experiencing menopausal symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cimicifuga racemosa herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Cimicifuga racemosa root, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Grind the Cimicifuga racemosa root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Cimicifuga racemosa powder, vitamin E oil, and lavender oil to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams contains a high concentration of bioactive constituents, including avenanthramides and beta-glucans. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin. Avenanthramides also possess antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The beta-glucans in Avena sativa creams can help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to hot flashes and night sweats during menopause. By reducing inflammation and regulating hormones, Avena sativa creams can provide relief from menopause symptoms, promoting a more comfortable and balanced lifestyle.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Avena sativa root, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Dry the Avena sativa root in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours or use a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried Avena sativa root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or blender.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Combine the melted coconut oil mixture, Avena sativa powder, vitamin E oil, and lavender essential oil in a bowl. Stir well and pour into a container. Let cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams contains active constituents like aucubin, rosmarinic acid, and agnuside, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats that often occur during menopause. The aucubin in Vitex agnus-castus creams has been shown to have a balancing effect on hormone levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of menopause such as mood swings and anxiety. The antioxidant properties of rosmarinic acid and agnuside can also help protect against cell damage caused by hormonal changes during menopause. By reducing inflammation and protecting cells, Vitex agnus-castus creams can provide relief from menopause symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of honey, 10 drops of Vitex agnus-castus essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and honey to the melted mixture. Stir until combined.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add Vitex agnus-castus essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let cool completely. Once set, the cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains saponins, specifically glycyrrhizin and flavonoids like licochalcone A, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to soothe skin irritations and reduce hot flashes associated with menopause. Glycyrrhizin also has a cortisol-balancing effect, which can help regulate mood swings and anxiety that often accompany menopause. Licochalcone A has antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and balancing cortisol levels, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from menopause symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of distilled water, 1/2 cup of glycerin, and 1/4 cup of glycyrrhiza glabra root extract in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the glycyrrhiza glabra root extract dissolves.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until the beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely. Label and store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. Gingerol has been shown to reduce hot flashes and night sweats in menopausal women. The creams also contain a compound called shogaol, which has been found to have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress. The combination of gingerol and shogaol in Zingiber officinale creams may help to alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, such as mood swings and fatigue. The anti-inflammatory properties of gingerol may also help to reduce joint pain and discomfort often experienced during menopause.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Stir in Zingiber officinale essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour mixture into small containers and let cool completely before use. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains bionactive constituents like rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and melissic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause. The rosmarinic acid in Melissa officinalis creams has a cooling effect on the body, reducing the sensation of heat and discomfort. The ursolic acid in the cream also has antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate cramps and muscle spasms that often occur during menopause. By reducing inflammation and soothing the body, Melissa officinalis creams can help provide relief from menopausal symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Melissa officinalis essential oil, 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in Melissa officinalis essential oil and jojoba oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar or tin and let it cool and solidify.
Once solid, your Melissa officinalis cream is ready to use. Apply it to the skin as needed to help alleviate menopause symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains active constituents like linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and calming properties. These compounds help to soothe and regulate the body's hormonal balance, which is often disrupted during menopause. The vasodilating effects of linalool and linalyl acetate can also help to improve blood flow and reduce hot flashes, a common symptom of menopause. Additionally, the sedative properties of camphor can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate menopause symptoms. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams topically, women can experience relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and other menopause-related symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, add the distilled water and stir well. Continue to heat until the mixture reaches 120-140°F (49-60°C).
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in the lavender essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains the active constituents naringenin, hyperforin, and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause by regulating hormonal imbalances and improving blood flow. The flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum creams also have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate anxiety and mood swings that often accompany menopause. Hyperforin, in particular, has been shown to have a positive effect on the body's natural hormone regulation, which can help to reduce the severity of menopause symptoms. By using Hypericum perforatum creams, women may experience relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and other symptoms associated with menopause.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a clean bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until it's warm and melted.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the coconut oil and stir until melted and well combined.
Add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the cream in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora creams contains paeoniflorin and paeonol as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats that often accompany menopause. The creams also contain paeonol, which has a sedative effect and can help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress associated with menopause. Paeoniflorin, on the other hand, has a cooling effect, which can help to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats. By reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and cooling the body, Paeonia lactiflora creams can provide relief from menopause symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Paeonia lactiflora herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup dried Paeonia lactiflora root, 2 cups water, 2 tablespoons honey, 2 tablespoons beeswax.
Combine Paeonia lactiflora root and water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the root. Let the liquid cool down.
In a separate bowl, mix honey and beeswax together until well combined. Add the cooled liquid to the honey-beeswax mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts. Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains the active constituents curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce hot flashes and night sweats that often accompany menopause. Curcumin's ability to block prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate hot flashes, makes it an effective remedy for this symptom. Additionally, demethoxycurcumin helps regulate estrogen levels, which can become imbalanced during menopause, leading to mood swings and other hormonal issues. Curcuma longa creams may also help alleviate joint pain and stiffness, a common complaint among menopausal women.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for menopause
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in the turmeric powder, jojoba oil, and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container or ice cube tray and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, scoop out the Curcuma longa cream and store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help some women manage menopause symptoms, but it won't stop it from happening. Herbal teas like black cohosh and red clover may ease hot flashes and mood changes, but they don't affect the hormone changes that cause menopause. They might just make it more bearable.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to ease menopause symptoms, but it's essential to consume them in moderation. Drinking them every day may lead to interactions with prescription medications or worsen conditions like high blood pressure. It's better to limit your daily intake to a few cups and monitor your body's response.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can be a natural way to help with menopause symptoms. The time it takes to notice results can vary from person to person. Some women may feel relief from hot flashes and mood swings within a few days to a week of drinking herbal tea. Others may take a few weeks to notice a difference.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for menopause symptoms can be most helpful in the evening, before bed. This can help calm hot flashes and night sweats, making it easier to fall asleep. Many women find that a warm cup of tea before bed helps them relax and feel more comfortable, leading to a better night's sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for menopause symptoms. For example, black cohosh might affect blood thinners or diabetes medications. St. John's Wort can interact with antidepressants and other medications. These interactions can change how your body reacts to medications, making them less effective or causing side effects.
faq_pregnant: It's generally not a good idea to drink herbal teas during pregnancy, especially if you're experiencing menopause symptoms. Some herbal teas can cause problems, like uterine contractions, which could affect your pregnancy. It's best to stick with plain water or other safe, pregnancy-friendly drinks.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, are okay in small amounts, but others, like blue cohosh and ginseng, might pass into breast milk and affect your baby. If you're unsure, try alternatives like ginger or raspberry leaf tea, which are often safe and might help with menopause symptoms.
title_2: 11 Effective Herbal Creams for Cancer Survivors Managing Menopause
